# 'repo sync' should not restack upstacks without --restack flag.
# This is the fix for issue #1134.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2024-12-28T18:14:15Z'

# setup
cd repo
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'

# set up a fake GitHub remote
shamhub init
shamhub new origin alice/example.git
shamhub register alice
git push origin main

env SHAMHUB_USERNAME=alice
gs auth login

# Create a stack: main -> feat1 -> feat2
git add feat1.txt
gs bc -m 'Add feature 1'
git add feat2.txt
gs cc -m 'Add feature 2'

gs ll
cmp stderr $WORK/golden/ll-before-sync.txt

# Submit both changes
gs branch submit --fill
stderr 'Created #1'
stderr 'Created #2'

# Merge feat1 (the downstack)
shamhub merge alice/example 1

# Sync without --restack
# This should delete feat1 and update feat2's base to main
# but NOT rebase feat2 onto main yet
gs repo sync
stderr '#1 was merged'

# feat2 should still exist but have metadata pointing to main
gs ll
cmp stderr $WORK/golden/ll-after-sync.txt

# Verify that feat2's base is now main in metadata
gs branch show feat2
stdout 'Base: main'

# Now explicitly restack to actually rebase
gs repo sync --restack

gs ll
cmp stderr $WORK/golden/ll-after-restack.txt

-- repo/feat1.txt --
feature 1
-- repo/feat2.txt --
feature 2

-- golden/ll-before-sync.txt --
┏━■ add-feature-2 (#2) ◀
┃   [0-9a-f]+ Add feature 2 \(now\)
┣━■ add-feature-1 (#1)
┃   [0-9a-f]+ Add feature 1 \(now\)
main
-- golden/ll-after-sync.txt --
┏━■ add-feature-2 (#2) ◀
┃   [0-9a-f]+ Add feature 2 \(now\)
main
-- golden/ll-after-restack.txt --
┏━■ add-feature-2 (#2) ◀
┃   [0-9a-f]+ Add feature 2 \(now\)
main
